description: GetTeamTotals does not guard against a null 'players' argument. If a caller passes null, the method throws NullReferenceException at the LINQ call rather than a descriptive ArgumentNullException. ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(players) should be added at the top of both GetTeamTotals and GetTeamAverages.
suggestedFix: Add 'ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(players);' as the first line of both GetTeamTotals and GetTeamAverages.

description: GetTeamAverages does not guard against a null 'players' argument. The empty-list guard (players.Count == 0) will itself throw NullReferenceException if players is null, rather than a descriptive ArgumentNullException.
suggestedFix: Add 'ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(players);' as the first line of GetTeamAverages, before the Count check.
